notes: Three bombs in tourist areas (Nelson restaurant, Aladdin café, Ghazala market); part of a documented mid-2000s pattern of Sinai tourist-town bombings (2004 Taba, 2005 Sharm El-Sheikh, both separate towns)

Rental prices increased notably; supermarket prices described as "steep compared to local wages" — worth weighing against the headline cheap-total-cost figure rather than treating "Dahab is cheap" as static

value_raw: Legal for non-Muslims/tourists in licensed hotels/bars/clubs/certain restaurants; genuinely relaxed in Dahab specifically as a tourist hub
notes: Ordinary supermarkets generally don't sell alcohol except designated tourist-hub stores (Drinkies, Cheers). Public drinking illegal. Meaningfully more available in Dahab than in Egypt generally

source_ref: [GAP — no specific temperature/humidity/rainfall figures sourced this pass]
notes: Known general pattern (hot, dry summers, mild winters, very low rainfall) but not verified with dated figures

fact_label: Dated terrorism incident inside Dahab since 2006
notes: A real, meaningful 20-year gap — but the honest limit is "not found this pass," not "confirmed zero incidents anywhere in South Sinai's tourist towns since" (2015 Metrojet, Sharm-specific, shows the broader region hasn't been incident-free)

value_raw: COVID-era accident of geography — Dahab was 'one of the few warm and open places' still accessible, seeding a persistent professional expat crowd
notes: Genuinely different formation story than either a decades-long retiree tradition (Belize's Ambergris Caye) or a recent visa-driven wave (South Africa's Remote Work Visa scene)

Dahab's own local facility (Dahab Medical Care Hospital) is prepared for diving-related injuries specifically; anything beyond routine/dive-specific care requires ambulance transfer to Sharm El-Sheikh, most severe cases medevac to Cairo. Roughly comparable in distance-shape to Belize's Corozal-to-Chetumal cross-border dependency, though within one country

value_raw: A real, double-edged community-texture fact — easy-to-join, low-barrier social scene, but a shallower, less stable long-term social fabric
notes: The same trade-off shape as Belize's Corozal retiree-churn pattern, though Dahab's churn reads as younger/shorter-stay rather than retirees leaving after a few years
Genuinely the weakest measured internet figures found across this dispatch's 11 new candidates, well below Cape Town's measured 48 Mbps
With drops during peak evening hours; practical guidance converges on treating a local 4G SIM as the real primary connection, not café WiFi
Distinct from the terrorism/insurgency findings above

value_raw: Dahab Medical Care Hospital (private, est. 1996 as Dahab Polyclinic) — inpatient treatment of severe infections, specialized physiotherapy, diving-injury care, dental, IV therapy
notes: No complex surgery, cardiology, or oncology capability found at this facility — genuinely routine/dive-specific-care tier, the bottom of the three-tier system documented in cost-of-living.md

fact_label: Location applicability — Sinai Peninsula foreign-ownership ban
notes: Confirmed directly — Dahab is explicitly named in PD 128/2022.

value_raw: For stays 3+ months, direct owner/agent negotiation is common given high turnover
notes: A genuinely different rental culture from a fixed-listing-price market
Same project-wide gap flagged everywhere else this session; what's available skews toward weeks-to-months digital-nomad-guide content

fact_label: Organized backlash against foreign remote workers/expats
source_ref: [GAP — not searched this pass, distinct from Belize's/Guatemala's dedicated passes]
notes: A reasoned expectation (less structurally likely given decades-long tourism-economy reliance on foreign spending) exists but is not a checked finding
Meaningfully cheaper than Belize's Corozal (this project's prior budget benchmark) and dramatically cheaper than Cape Town's ~$2,092/month. Neither figure's methodology independently verified
The actual current texture of risk in the town itself — genuinely more mundane than the regional terrorism history might imply on its own
The smallest population of any location researched in this project to date, smaller than any of Belize's four candidate towns

source_ref: [GAP — no property.md exists for Egypt; self-filed open task]
notes: Given the Sinai-specific freehold ban, a real property.md would need to document leasehold pricing, not a per-m² freehold figure
A separate, unreconciled $189/month estimate also exists from the same aggregator source — a real, named conflict between two figures, not silently picked

value_raw: Named consistently as the cheapest of Dahab's areas
notes: Lighthouse commands a premium as the town's most nomad/expat-dense cluster

value_raw: No precedent found or searched for this pass; any group scenario faces the same leasehold-only constraint an individual purchase does
notes: A structural, not just social, question for this lens, per the Sinai-specific freehold ban above
Not approved for operation; unlicensed use is illegal, hardware import restricted. More categorically blocked than Belize's contested-but-live Starlink fight

value_raw: A genuinely dive-anchored community, not a generic remote-work crowd near a beach — social and recreational geography are the same map
notes: The Lighthouse area's role as nomad/expat center of gravity is directly tied to its proximity to the best shore-diving reef
Distinct from Belize's dedicated (if unresolved) treatment of the same question

value_raw: Illegal; personal-use possession min. 1yr imprisonment + fines from 1,000 EGP; cultivation w/ trafficking intent up to death penalty
notes: Anti-Narcotics Law No. 182/1960 (amended 1989) — the harshest cannabis regime found in this project to date. No leniency for foreign nationals; CBD also illegal. A structural, hard-blocker-tier risk

2026 outlook shifted from crisis-adjustment to managed stabilization — projected USD/EGP ~51-52 early 2026, ~53-54 by year-end, a controlled depreciation path. Real, live risk, distinct from and larger than Cape Town's floating-Rand risk

fact_label: Expat private health insurance — annual cost range
notes: Varies by age, health status, coverage level; broader international plans with medevac/repatriation cost more. Local/regional-plan figure, not a full international-plan figure

value_raw: $27B debt service due 2026, vs. projected $15B current-account deficit
notes: Interest payments now exceed half of total government expenditure — a real structural fiscal-fragility backdrop, even though the currency trajectory is improving

value_raw: Exists in principle, tied to marriage to an Egyptian national
notes: No Egypt-specific mechanics/thresholds/timeline researched this pass — a real gap distinct from Belize's fully-mapped marriage-track research
Central Bank of Egypt guiding toward a 5-9% medium-term target

value_raw: Multiple internationally accredited (JCI) tertiary centers (As-Salam International, Saudi German Cairo, Dar Al Fouad, Andalusia Al Maadi) — cardiac, oncology, transplant, neurology capability
notes: Genuine complex/tertiary capability exists in-country, just not locally in Dahab or Sharm — the medevac destination for the most severe cases per red-flags.md

fact_label: No live digital-nomad or remote-work visa exists
notes: No long-term visa route built around passive/retirement income either, unlike Belize's Qualified Retired Persons program (QRP) or South Africa's Retired Person's Visa

UK Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office (FCDO) "do not travel" advisory for North Sinai; ISIS-Sinai Province insurgency running over a decade. Attacks on security forces "still occur but are becoming increasingly rare" per one 2026 source — real but geographically distinct from Dahab's own South Sinai coastal zone

value_raw: 14-day grace period, then ~1,685 EGP (+ admin fees) for first 3 months, escalating
notes: A relatively modest penalty structure compared to Belize's BZ$5,000/2-years-imprisonment statutory ceiling; exact escalation beyond 3 months not itemized

value_raw: Same rabies/microchip timing as dog, but vaccination panel is feline viral rhinotracheitis/calicivirus/panleukopenia instead
notes: A sixth genuine exception to this project's dog-only pet-import gap. No specific quarantine requirement found for either species — treat as an open question, not confirmed "no quarantine" the way Belize's research explicitly confirmed

value_raw: Rabies vaccine 30 days–11 months before arrival, no titer required; also distemper/hepatitis/parvovirus/parainfluenza
notes: ISO microchip mandatory, must be inserted BEFORE rabies vaccination (specific sequencing requirement)

value_raw: President Sisi's government reads as stable/continuous authoritarian-leaning system, no named 2026 transition event found
notes: Not independently verified against a dedicated political-stability source — a real gap relative to Belize's/Cape Town's more thorough treatment

source_ref: [GAP — no Egypt-specific figure found or confirmed this pass]
notes: The insurers expats in Egypt actually use (AXA, Allianz, Cigna Global, Bupa) carry a general-industry new-enrollee ceiling commonly landing 65-75 (Cigna Global: 75; Aetna International: 74; IMG Global: 80) — a realistic operating assumption, not a confirmed Egypt-specific figure

Reported $700 cost, ~180 countries eligible; no application process, eligibility detail, or effective date published. Same "coming, not here" shape as Belize's Cabinet-approved-but-unenacted investor fast-track

fact_label: Public healthcare quality (independent of foreigner eligibility)
value_raw: Underfunded, dilapidated infrastructure, outdated equipment, long waits, insufficient staff/sanitation — named complaints even for citizens
notes: Standing expat guidance converges on avoiding public facilities regardless of formal eligibility

fact_label: Public Universal Health Insurance System — foreigner eligibility
value_raw: Optional; gated on reciprocal agreement with home country OR formal Egyptian payroll employment
notes: Law No. 2/2018, rolling out in phases, primarily targeted at Egyptian citizens. Not a realistic door for someone with no Egyptian employer and no reciprocal-agreement home country — functionally private-only in practice

source_ref: [GAP — the single highest-priority open question in Egypt's file]
notes: Whether a 99-year Sinai leasehold (the only instrument foreigners can hold in Dahab) qualifies as the "investment" this program requires, or whether it requires freehold title specifically (unavailable in Dahab) — every source describing the program cites documentation language that reads as assuming freehold ownership

value_raw: Proof of medical insurance policy + health certificate/medical exam from an approved Egyptian hospital required as part of the application
notes: A compound gate on top of the property-investment threshold itself — relevant given the route's own unresolved leasehold-eligibility question for Dahab specifically (see visa-legal.md)
A genuinely operational program, renewable while ownership is maintained; does not require personally occupying the unit (can be rented). Extends to spouse and children

fact_label: Real-Estate-Investment Residency converts to permanent residency
notes: A renewable temporary-residence mechanism tied to continued ownership, not a direct route to full PR
Direct freehold, capped at 2 units nationally, each ≤4,000 m² — an open regime with a scale cap, not a band or nationality gate.
Law 14/2012 (general Sinai ban), tightened by Presidential Decree 128/2022 excluding Sharm El-Sheikh/Dahab/Gulf of Aqaba sector specifically.

value_raw: Sharm El-Sheikh International Hospital — nationally accredited and internationally accredited (JCI), 24hr ED, general surgery/cardiology/orthopedics/OB-GYN/pediatrics/neurology/radiology/internal medicine
notes: The standard transfer destination for anything beyond Dahab's own routine/dive-specific care per red-flags.md's distance-to-emergency-care row; distance/travel-time itself is excluded from this criterion's score by definition

value_raw: 'All but essential travel' advisory beyond the St Catherine-Nuweibaa road; does NOT cover the coastal areas/Dahab itself
notes: An inland-desert-interior warning — any off-route inland excursion (desert safaris, Mount Sinai treks) needs checking against this boundary separately from Dahab's own coastal "low risk" status
Egypt's worldwide-vs-territorial tax-system status not confirmed — flagged as a follow-up priority

fact_label: Tenure mechanism — direct freehold, capped (national, outside Sinai)

notes: Restricted to Egyptian nationals/entities with entirely Egyptian capital.
Described as secure/legally-recognized/widely-used by developers; not independently checked against first-hand accounts this pass.
Single-entry e-visa valid 90 days for use (stay still capped at 30 days); multi-entry e-visa valid 180 days. Visa-on-arrival available to ~50 nationalities for $30 USD cash

value_raw: Additional 30 days available in Egypt; discretionary range of 30-90 days reported depending on circumstances
notes: Fee ~$20-30. Processed at Abbassia, Cairo, or (closer to Dahab) Alexandria/Hurghada/Sharm El Sheikh/Luxor. The discretion-based range is itself real uncertainty, not a firm number
Not researched to full mechanical depth this pass
